seoTitle: Wells Crossing vs Wentworth — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Wells Crossing and Wentworth. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Wells Crossing vs Wentworth: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wells Crossing and Wentworth.
heading: Wells Crossing vs Wentworth — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wells Crossing, New South Wales and Wentworth, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wentworth has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Wells Crossing (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wells Crossing or Wentworth safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wells Crossing has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Wentworth scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wells Crossing scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Wentworth scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wells Crossing or Wentworth?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wentworth has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
